vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by visual evidence. P1 (Yellow Pot) and P2/P4 (Black Pot) appear devoid of their assigned specimens. P3 (Black Pot) contains the white rabbit anchor but lacks the Pothos foliage.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No String of Nickels visible; soil appears bare.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No Mexican Mint visible.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss: Pothos absent; only white rabbit anchor remains.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface, no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface, no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; single green sprout/stem visible in the lower quadrant of the pot.
plant_audit.P4: Integrated into P2/P3 space; no distinct specimen identified.

biome_observations.incidental_growth: One singular, thin green sprout identified in P3.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Severe lack of biomass compared to registry. The environment is essentially a collection of empty containers.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-1: Minimal change; persistent absence of foliage.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: The single sprout in P3 remains static; no new growth or decay observed in the last 24 hours.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of near-total collapse. The only remaining biological activity is a single, isolated sprout in P3. All other registered specimens are presumed dead or removed.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a single, unidentifiable sprout in P3 is the only deviation from a completely empty set of pots.
vision_report.narrative_description: Upon meticulous audit, the biome registry is found to be functionally obsolete. The pots are largely barren, suggesting either a recent clearing of the desk or a catastrophic failure of the specimens. The white rabbit anchor in P3 is the only consistent landmark. A single, thin green sprout is the sole occupant of the entire desk, representing the only potential for ongoing growth.
